concerns.text: T015 mandates raising an explicit exception if download fails, directly contradicting spec.md (FR-001, US-1) which requires an automatic fallback to synthetic data generation when no linked public dataset exists. This is a semantic weakening of the requirement.

T026 forbids simulation for object counting, mandating a 'real model' (YOLOv5n). This risks violating spec.md (SC-008) and plan.md (Performance Goals) regarding the 6-hour runtime limit on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 CPU) for N≥100 images, as the spec allows for CPU-tractable methods but does not guarantee the specific model will meet the time budget without a fallback.

T019 creates a separate 'cv_methods.md' file for citations. However, spec.md (SC-002, SC-005) requires that the *output documentation* and *statistical tables* themselves use associational language and include specific p-value tables. A separate file does not satisfy the requirement that the analysis results in the final report are framed correctly.

T036 generates a 'multiplicity_table.csv' but does not explicitly link this artifact to the final 'statistics.json' or the report generation step. spec.md (SC-005) requires a summary table of exact and adjusted p-values to be generated as part of the final output evidence; T036 risks creating a disconnected intermediate artifact.

T016 tasks synthetic image generation but lacks explicit instructions to generate and record the required metadata (lighting_condition, room_type) alongside the images. This violates spec.md (US-1) and plan.md (Constitution Principle VII) which mandate metadata on lighting, layout, and demographics to support generalizability assessments.
